posts.title: Nobel Mentor for 26 Years Changed Everything
posts.ig_caption:
I went there, asked him, gave me his email address, became my mentor for years.

For 26 years, he's been sparking new ideas on every single call.

The man who discovered gene splicing — and why one question changed everything.

posts.title: Gut Feeling Is Actually Science
posts.ig_caption:
Some people say that gut feeling is very mystical, but I think that's wrong.
It's not magic — it's your brain processing more than words can explain.
Science and intuition aren't opposites. They might be the same thing.
Full episode — link in bio 🎙️

#MedicalInnovation #GutFeeling #DecisionScience #HealthcareLeadership #MedTech #MedicalPodcast

posts.title: Patient's Reaction to AI Stethoscope
posts.ig_caption:
The doctor turns it on, the LED lights go on — and the patient says, God damn.

That one reaction said everything.

When patients go from skeptical to stunned — you know the tech is doing something right.

Others see a moat.

posts.title: Doctor Couldn't Hear the Wheeze
posts.ig_caption:
Diana had a terrible shift — a patient nearly died because the doctor couldn't hear the wheeze.

A severe asthma crisis. A patient brought in too late. A doctor who missed the warning signs his ears should have caught.

What if the technology to prevent this already exists?

Full episode — link in bio 🎙️

#MedicalInnovation #DigitalHealth #AIinHealthcare #FutureOfMedicine #HealthTech

The real value of a consultation isn't just words. It's data you can't ignore.

posts.title: Hardware Is a Moat Nobody Wants
posts.ig_caption:
Every single piece of regulatory achievement becomes a valuation price point for your company.

Most founders see FDA clearance as a hurdle. The smart ones see it as a moat.

Jhonatan Bringas breaks down why hardware in medtech isn't a liability — it's leverage.

posts.title: NICU Nurses Leave It in Incubators
posts.ig_caption:
We have nurses in NICUs using it — and they leave it inside the incubators.

When a device is trusted enough to stay in a neonatal incubator, that says everything.

Doctors and nurses are calling it safe, portable, and game-changing for the tiniest patients.

Full episode — link in bio 🎙️

#MedicalInnovation #NICU #AIStethoscope #PediatricCare #HealthTech #MedicalDevices

posts.title: This Is the iPhone Moment for Medicine
posts.ig_caption:
Converging phone, MP3 player, and camera into one device — that's a little bit our strategy.

What Steve Jobs did for tech in 2007, medicine might be about to experience.

One device. Three technologies. A category that doesn't exist yet.

Full episode — link in bio 🎙️

posts.title: Devices Stuck in Customs, Clock Ticking
posts.ig_caption:
The devices were in the airport in Peru, stuck in customs — and we were supposed to start in a week.

When a medical mission is on the line, you do whatever it takes.

Dr. Jhonatan Bringas flew to South America, navigated the legal maze, and got it done — just in time.

Full episode — link in bio 🎙️

#MedicalInnovation #HealthTech #AIStethoscope #Cardiology #MedicalDevices #HealthcareLeadership

posts.ig_caption:

Two doctors. One mission. A life-changing outcome they never saw coming.

Sometimes doing good leads to something greater — maybe it's karma.

posts.title: Wireless Charging Keeps Hospitals Safer
posts.ig_caption:
It has no USB-C plug — and that was a very intentional decision.

In hospitals, every port is a potential hiding spot for microbes.

This design choice could change how we think about medical device safety.

Full episode — link in bio 🎙️

#MedicalInnovation #HealthcareDesign #AIStethoscope #InfectionControl #MedTech #DigitalHealth
